title: How to end dynamic browser feature changes?
content:
As an older person, having to learn and relearn and constantly relearn how my browser functions is becoming more and more aggravating frustrating and put off ish.

Back in the day, these things didnt change automatically, another version was released instead for download with build notes.

It is starting to feel like having a computer and using it is becoming a troll. Im being serious.

One day the tabs are smaller on the left side, the next week the tabs are somehow grouping together and even though I didnt ask for it to happen, nor did I make it happen, its happening!. Now I have a mute button on the tab, even though my computer already 9 different mute buttons.

One day I had my grand daughter over and it took me 45 minutes to realize why there was no audio on netflix for her to watch. These constant feature changes are being a major part of my and everybodys lives. We are all becoming more impatient with these things.

The majority of new features seem to be unused, from my perspective, by the majority of comments I read on here, facebook, x, etc, they are just simply jammed down our collective throats seemingly at the whims of a designer thinking to themselves "I'm going to move this button over here!" This stuff is not making our worlds better. This stuff is not making our lives easier. This stuff is not making our lives simpler.

The effect is the exact opposite. I read a story last month about an autistic teenager that had a cell phone, because everyone else has a cell phone. When they couldn't get their phone to do whatever it was they wanted it do, they threw it in frustration. Turns out they broke a window at school that cost $5000 by throwing the phone. i don't know what app this person was using or attempted to use, and what caused the frustration, but I sure feel that pain sometimes. The parent posted that because the school sent them a bill for the window replacement.

Let me repeat that: The parent received a $5000 invoice for a window their child broke at school, because they were frustrated with phone.

Is there a way to limit the feature changes or stop them while maintaining security updates?

I literally find myself using the internet less and less, and I feel like this has a lot to do with it. As your product becomes more and frustrating to use, I am using it less and less. And I know thats not what anyone wants.

Why can't we have the best of both worlds??

title: Firefox forcefully crashing browser/entire PC to update???
content:
I use a laptop that's estimated to be around 5 years old now, it still has good performance and rarely shuts down/crashes. The only performance issues it has are with Firefox, as it eats the most CPU and memory of anything else I use. Twice or so now, the following has happened within the past month: - Firefox notifies me that a new version is available and asks to update to it - I decline to do because I have tabs on there with unsaved/algorithmic work I'm in the middle of dealing with, and the browser update would mean losing all of it (or I just don't care) - At some point later, be it a few days or hours later, Firefox suddenly seizes up and forces me to close the application, after which it updates to the newest version.

While it's always frustrating to have this happen, it's generally pretty quick and I can get back to whatever I was doing (as my tabs reload). But today was different. Very different.

So, imagine. You're slacking off, watching some random videos online, and your screen (and eventually audio, too) freeze up. You get Windows to shut it down as it detects the application has gone cold, and it goes and tries to "report the issue", except that little loading screen doesn't seem to come to a stop. "Okay..." So, you click "X", and it goes away after a few tries, but the Firefox icon is still on your taskbar like it's still active. You click it, it doesn't do anything. You click on your other open applications, they also don't do anything. Suddenly, your entire desktop freaks out! You can't ALT+F4 (none of your keys work in fact), the screen is randomly turning black for fractions of a second. Only when you hold down the power button for what feels like a whole minute as a last ditch effort do you get your potato of a computer to go lights out. You press down on the power button again, it boots back up fine. Firefox reopens, and it gives you a little popup about pinning it to your taskbar.

Did Firefox literally crash my computer... to tell me I can use a free (and probably useless) VPN now?... IS THIS WHAT YOU WANTED, YOU LITTLE VULPINE BASTARD??!?!?!

I don't why it's acting like some immature incinerated furry spawn of internet Satan because I simply "decline" to update the browser, but it's really getting on my nerves. I have NEVER HAD an application, let alone a memory-munching browser like this, cause my entire PC to freeze to a severity I have not seen in a long time if ever in the history of using it. What is making it wreak havoc on my computer all for a stupid update that adds garbage I never use? How do I make it stop? Why has it been such an ass with me recently???

I can't let this keep happening, man. It's already caused me to lose some important stuff before, and now apparently it may be able to screw me over on other applications as well. Something could go seriously wrong on my end, and then boom, I move back to Chrome because despite everything, it treated me with more decency when it came to performance. Do better.
creator: GrahamFitnessCrackerTest

title: My developer account has been disabled and I want to appeal
content:
I am the developer of FastF1, an open source Python package for downloading and analyzing Formula 1 race results, timing data and telemetry. The package is widely used in the fan community for independent analysis of results. For some advanced features, the addon requires users to authenticate with their Formula1/F1TV account. Since their services don't have an open login API, a companion addon is required to allow users to sign in using their browser and the official website.

I have just noticed that my developer account has been banned and the addon was blocked in Firefox. I have not received any email about this and don't know why exactly this decision was made. I'd like appeal this and reinstate my account and the addon, but I don't know how to proceed from here.

FastF1 and the companion addon are fully open source and intend no harm to users. https://github.com/theOehrly/Fast-F1 https://github.com/theOehrly/fastf1-companion

I hope this problem can be resolved.
creator: oehrly

title: Websites not loading due to error message "This web site requires a secure connection."
content:
Whenever i try to connect to any site, it fails to load and displays the following error message:

This web site requires a secure connection. - The page you are trying to view cannot be shown because this web site requires a secure connection. - The issue is most likely with the web site, and there is nothing you can do to resolve it. - You can notify the web site’s administrator about the problem.

When i press 'advanced' it says: [Site name] has a security policy called HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS), which means that Firefox can only connect to it securely. You can’t add an exception to visit this site.

This happens on every site i try to visit. I am on a samsung phone running android version 15. Other browsers do not have this problem (i am sending this from opera), and firefox works fine on my other devices.
